questions.question: A patient is prescribed $2 \cdot \sqrt{5}$ milligrams of medication. If $\sqrt{5} \approx 2.24$, how many milligrams of medication will the patient take?
The volume of a cell is increasing at a rate of $2 \cdot \sqrt{t}$ cubic millimeters per hour. If $t$ is the number of hours, what is the volume of the cell after 4 hours?
The probability of a patient responding to a new medication is $\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$. What is the probability that the patient will not respond to the medication?

A scientist is studying the growth of a cell culture. The number of cells is increasing at a rate of $3 \cdot \sqrt{t}$ cells per hour. If $t$ is the number of hours, what is the number of cells after 9 hours?

The length of a microscope slide is $2 \cdot \sqrt{5}$ centimeters. If the width of the slide is $x$ centimeters, and the area of the slide is $20 \sqrt{5}$ square centimeters, find the value of $x$. Show all work and simplify your answer.

The volume of a cell is given by the formula $V = \frac{4}{3} \pi r^3$, where $r$ is the radius of the cell. If the radius of the cell is increasing at a rate of $2 \cdot \sqrt{t}$ millimeters per hour, find the rate at which the volume of the cell is increasing after 4 hours. Show all work and simplify your answer.

teacher_rules: Ensure students show all work and simplify their answers.
Emphasize the importance of checking units in word problems.
Encourage students to use calculators to check their work, but not to rely solely on them.
